brief_summary: Hero art for the guides-pillar buyer's guide on choosing a first barebones custom keyboard kit (case + plate + PCB, not a full board). Motif: exploded-view stacked layers — case shell, plate, PCB — with dashed explode-gap dividers, downward assembly arrows, alignment guides tying standoff/mounting-hole positions across the three layers, and a faint ghost silhouette at the bottom showing the assembled result. Conveys 'kit of parts you assemble' rather than a finished keyboard.
source: plan/bearings.md § Article hero art — colorful keyboard SVGs (locked 2026-05-09)
template: directive: simple line drawing + single splash of color illustrative of subject; guide default is an annotated motif — reinterpreted here as an exploded kit-parts stack (case/plate/PCB) since that is the article's literal subject matter
subject: Exploded-view diagram of a barebones keyboard kit: a case-shell silhouette (top layer) with standoff posts, a switch plate (middle layer) with cutouts and mounting holes, and a PCB (bottom layer) with a connector notch, trace hints, and mounting holes — all three registered to the same x-positions via faint dashed alignment guides. Downward arrows mark the explode gaps as assembly steps. A faint dashed ghost silhouette below shows the finished, assembled case with a single ochre accent carried through as the visual through-line.
